title: zornia dyctiocarpa var. dyctiocarpa
intro: Zornia dyctiocarpa var. dyctiocarpa, also known as Mexican clammyclover, is a plant species in the legume family (Fabaceae) that is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that are believed to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, providing various health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. Zornia dyctiocarpa var. dyctiocarpa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some herbal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
